questions.q: What is the primary difference between object storage and block storage?
options: Object storage is faster but block storage is more reliable
Block storage is attached to compute instances as volumes; object storage is accessed via HTTP API and stores complete files
Object storage requires a filesystem; block storage does not
Block storage can only store 1 TB while object storage is unlimited
questions.explanation: Block storage (like EBS) attaches volumes to EC2 instances for high-performance I/O, while object storage (like S3) is accessed via API for storing complete files or objects. Option 0 is false; option 2 is reversed; option 3 incorrectly specifies limits.

questions.q: What does storage durability in cloud storage mean?
options: How quickly data can be retrieved from storage
The percentage probability that your data will not be lost over a specified period
The encryption strength applied to data at rest
How many copies of your data are kept in the same data center
questions.explanation: Durability is the probability that data remains intact and accessible over time. AWS S3 offers 99.999999999% (11 nines) durability. Option 0 is availability/performance; option 2 is security; option 3 is replication strategy, not durability.

questions.q: What is the purpose of storage tiers in cloud storage like S3?
options: To separate data by file type (images, videos, documents)
To provide different levels of access speed and cost for different use cases
To automatically delete old data after a specified period
To enforce different encryption standards for different data
questions.explanation: Storage tiers (Standard, Infrequent Access, Glacier, Deep Archive) optimize the cost/performance tradeoff based on access patterns. Frequent access uses Standard; infrequent or archival use cheaper tiers. Option 0 is about content; option 2 is lifecycle; option 3 is encryption policy.

questions.q: Which storage tier is most appropriate for frequently accessed data?
options: Glacier Deep Archive
S3 Intelligent-Tiering
S3 Standard
Glacier Flexible Retrieval
questions.explanation: S3 Standard provides the fastest access and is appropriate for hot data accessed frequently. Glacier tiers (options 0 and 3) are for long-term archive; Intelligent-Tiering (option 1) automates tier transitions based on usage patterns.

questions.q: What is a Content Delivery Network (CDN) and why is it used with cloud storage?
options: A service that encrypts data before storing it in the cloud
A distributed network of edge servers that cache and serve content from locations closer to end users
A backup system that replicates data across multiple regions
A load balancer that distributes storage requests across multiple servers
questions.explanation: A CDN like CloudFront caches content at edge locations worldwide, serving data from the server nearest to the user for reduced latency. Option 0 is encryption; option 2 is replication/backup; option 3 is load balancing.

questions.q: What is file storage in cloud environments?
options: Storage that uses block storage underneath with a filesystem interface for multiple instances
A cheaper alternative to object storage for storing large files
Storage that only works with NFS protocol
A backup solution for database files only
questions.explanation: File storage (like EFS) provides shared filesystem access across multiple compute instances using NFS. Option 1 is wrong about cost; option 2 is too restrictive (EFS supports multiple protocols); option 3 is too narrow (all file types are supported).

questions.q: When should you choose object storage over file storage?
options: Only when your application is web-based
When you need to share a filesystem across multiple instances
When storing complete, unstructured data like images, videos, or logs accessed via API
Only for backups and archival purposes
questions.explanation: Object storage excels for unstructured data accessed via API, like web assets, logs, and backups. Option 0 is too restrictive; option 1 describes file storage; option 3 is too narrow (object storage is for general use).

questions.q: What is meant by storage replication in cloud storage?
options: Making multiple copies of data within the same data center
Compressing data to reduce storage costs
Distributing copies of data across multiple geographic locations for fault tolerance and disaster recovery
Creating backup snapshots at scheduled intervals
questions.explanation: Replication copies data across geographic regions or AZs to protect against data center failures. Option 0 describes within-region redundancy but not geographic distribution; option 1 is compression; option 3 is snapshots.

questions.q: What is the lifecycle policy in cloud storage services?
options: The encryption algorithm applied to data
The maximum retention period before data is automatically deleted
A rule that automatically transitions data between storage tiers or deletes it based on age or access patterns
The approval process for granting access to stored data
questions.explanation: Lifecycle policies automate data management by transitioning objects between tiers (e.g., Standard to Glacier after 30 days) or deleting them based on age. Option 0 is encryption; option 1 is retention only; option 3 is access control.

questions.q: What advantage does cloud storage provide over on-premises storage?
options: Cloud storage is always faster than local storage
Cloud storage requires less security configuration
Cloud storage offers elasticity, managed redundancy, global availability, and pay-as-you-go pricing without upfront hardware costs
Cloud storage eliminates the need for backups
questions.explanation: Cloud storage provides flexibility, automatic redundancy, geographic distribution, and cost efficiency without purchasing infrastructure. Option 0 is false (latency depends on access patterns); option 1 is false (security is still critical); option 3 is false (backups are still important).
